summary: Integration tests for SwarmForge CLI - 8 scenarios that force wiring all components together. The key gap: start.Run() only does tmux session creation but does NOT call preflight, setup, banner, prompt generation, agent launching, or metrics pane initialization. There is no real Commander implementation and no cmd/swarmforge/main.go.
instructions:
Read features/swarmforge-integration.feature (8 scenarios). Write acceptance tests in internal/acceptance/swarmforge_integration_test.go. These tests MUST force the following changes to be made by the Coder:

1. EXPAND start.Config to include: FS (setup.FS), LookPath (preflight.LookPathFunc), ConstitutionPath (string), Stdout (io.Writer)

2. EXPAND start.Run() to call the FULL sequence:
   a. preflight.Check(cfg.LookPath, 'tmux', 'claude', 'watch')
   b. setup.EnsureDirs(cfg.FS, cfg.ProjectRoot)
   c. setup.WriteHelperScripts(cfg.FS, cfg.ProjectRoot)
   d. Read constitution file via cfg.FS.ReadFile(cfg.ConstitutionPath)
   e. banner.Print(cfg.Stdout)
   f. Kill existing session if present
   g. Create tmux session + split grid + set pane titles
   h. Generate 3 agent prompts via prompt.Build() and write them to temp files via cfg.FS.WriteFile
   i. Launch 3 agents via tmux.LaunchAgent() in panes 0, 1, 2
   j. Initialize metrics pane 3 with tail -f command

3. CREATE a real Commander implementation (e.g. tmux.ExecCommander) that wraps os/exec.Command('tmux', args...)

4. CREATE cmd/swarmforge/main.go that:
   - Creates a real Commander, real FS, real LookPath
   - Wires cli.Dispatch with handler functions that call start.Run, notify.Notify, swarmlog.Write
   - Builds successfully with 'go build ./cmd/swarmforge/'

Your acceptance tests should use RecordingCommander and FakeFS stubs (reuse or extend from existing tests) to verify the EXACT sequence of calls. The critical test is Scenario 1 which verifies the complete startup sequence.

After writing tests, create agent_context/handoff-to-coder.json and notify pane 2.
